question: Looking at the US population map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The population differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.
options.B: Kansas and Indiana have very similar population levels.
options.C: All the highlighted states have extremely diverse population levels.
options.D: The population gap between Kansas and Indiana might exceed that between Oklahoma and Louisiana.

explanation: Kansas (25.3M) and Indiana (29.0M) have a 3.7M population difference, while Oklahoma (11.6M) and Louisiana (12.5M) have only a 0.9M difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Kansas and Indiana into the same color bin (20M+ range), making their large difference appear similar, while Oklahoma and Louisiana appear more different due to different color bins.
